opinion.h4: HASWELL v. POWELL, SECRETARY OF STATE OF ILLINOIS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 1055. Decided April 22, 1968.
opinion.p: William G. Clark, Attorney General of Illinois, and John J. O'Toole and Robert F. Nix, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: TIMES MIRROR CO. v. UNITED STATES, 390 U.S. 712 (1968)
opinion.p: 390 U.S. 712 TIMES MIRROR CO. v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE CENTRAL DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 1162. Decided April 22, 1968.
Julian O. von Kalinowski for appellant.
Solicitor General Griswold, Assistant Attorney General Turner and Bernard M. Hollander for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN would note probable jurisdiction and set the case for oral argument.
